extractions.claim: China-US air freight rates spiked 50% in April 2025 ahead of de minimis deadline, then collapsed
extractions.evidence: In the rush to move air cargo before the May 2 de minimis deadline, the rate from Hong Kong to the US in mid-April shot up 50% compared with the previous week to $7.95/kg. In February 2025, when de minimis was briefly removed, spot rates from Shanghai to US dropped 29% month-on-month.

verification.verifier_notes: From Bertling air freight market reports

extractions.claim: E-commerce accounted for 50%+ of trans-Pacific air freight shipments in 2024; ~100K tons/month before de minimis end
extractions.evidence: E-commerce shipments accounted for more than 50% of trans-Pacific air freight shipments in 2024. By the end of April 2025, around 100,000 tons a month of de minimis-type traffic was moving across the Pacific on more than 60 freighters dedicated to carrying e-commerce.

verification.verifier_notes: From aviation industry reports and S&P Global

extractions.claim: Global air cargo spot rates down just 1% YoY to $2.56/kg in January 2026; NE Asia to NA down 3% YoY
extractions.evidence: Global air cargo spot rates were down just 1% year-on-year to USD 2.56 per kg in January 2026. Northeast Asia to North America saw only a modest 3% year-on-year decline.

verification.verifier_notes: From Freightos and stattimes.com air freight analysis

extractions.claim: Chinese e-commerce platforms pivoting to European market to offset US volume losses
extractions.evidence: With the US market increasingly hostile to their air-freight-dependent business model, Chinese cross-border e-commerce giants are accelerating their pivot toward Europe, where Chinese e-commerce activity has been growing to offset the higher cost of importing goods into the United States.

verification.verifier_notes: Structural shift confirmed by multiple freight industry sources
